stories.headline: Massacre at Deep Creek: The Slaughter the Canyon Hid for a Century
stories.dek: In 1887, at least 31 Chinese gold miners were murdered in Hells Canyon. No one was ever convicted.

stories.headline: Three Days Apart: The Twin Railroad Avalanches of 1910
stories.dek: Seventy-two hours after Wellington, the Selkirks took 58 more men — the deadliest week the high iron ever knew.

stories.headline: White Death at Wellington
stories.dek: March 1, 1910 — an avalanche swept two Great Northern trains into a canyon, killing 96. The deadliest avalanche in U.S. history.

stories.headline: The Deschutes Canyon Railroad War
stories.dek: Dynamite, sharpshooters, and hand-to-hand combat as two railroad titans warred up Oregon's Deschutes Canyon.

stories.headline: The Night He Walked Alone: John F. Stevens' Discovery of Marias Pass
stories.dek: Alone in a Montana blizzard, John F. Stevens found the pass that completed a transcontinental dream.

stories.headline: Stampede Pass: The $1,000 Race Through the Mountain
stories.dek: Two crews bored a mountain from opposite sides, racing for a $1,000 prize and the future of Puget Sound.

stories.headline: The Great Big Baked Potato
stories.dek: How the Northern Pacific turned an Idaho spud into the most famous meal on American rails.
